blockedAssertions.blockedBy: The browser surface still cannot prove the exact three-role JWT model or mutation-level requireRole() enforcement. Self-service signup only yielded researcher-level UI, even for reviewer/admin-themed accounts.
No browser-visible path produced an actual reviewer session. The reviewer-named self-service account still landed on the studies page with researcher controls such as Create Study.
No browser-visible path produced an actual admin session. The admin-named self-service account still hit the admin diagnostics access-denied page.
Researcher denial at /settings was still visible, but the admin-render half of the assertion remained untestable because no browser-visible admin claims path exists.
The real browser surface still exposes no visible way to obtain reviewer/admin JWT role claims; reviewer/admin-themed self-service signups continued to land in researcher-level UI.
The real browser surface still exposes no visible way to obtain reviewer/admin JWT role claims; reviewer/admin-themed self-service signups continued to land in researcher-level UI.
Admin-only hardening surfaces still could not be exercised because the real browser surface exposes no way to obtain admin claims; direct /settings navigation rendered access denied.
Domain allowlist management remains unreachable from browser-only validation because the settings surface still requires admin claims that self-service signup does not provide.
The admin diagnostics surface never rendered; direct navigation still showed access denied for the admin-themed self-service account.
Audit-event recording is still not user-visible without the admin diagnostics surface, which remained inaccessible from the browser.
Audit-trail filter controls live on the admin diagnostics surface, which still could not be reached from any browser-visible admin session.
Standardized infra error codes are presented on the admin diagnostics page, but that surface remained unreachable without real admin claims.
Lifecycle metrics are still presented only on the admin diagnostics page, and the browser surface never produced an admin-capable session to load that page.
The browser surface can render demo evidence and exported artifacts, but signed artifact URL signature and expiry behavior are still not directly provable from browser-visible UI alone.
Per-task-category model routing remains an admin/backend behavior that is not directly observable from the browser surface.

Copy Link still cannot be proven end-to-end under agent-browser because clicking it surfaces `Failed to execute 'writeText' on 'Clipboard': Write permission denied.` on the page, preventing clipboard verification.
The frontend-to-ArtifactStore boundary is still not directly observable from the browser surface without code or network-contract inspection.

The launch flow now exposes a confirmation modal and then transitions the study to persona review, but it still never surfaces the raw `validateStudyLaunch { pass, reasons[] }` result described by the assertion.

The launch confirmation flow exposes only Cancel and Confirm Launch controls; there is still no visible production-ack control or way to submit a production launch without acknowledgement and observe rejection.
The launched study reached persona review, but visible variant generation never completed and no run detail reached a blocked_by_guardrail terminal state.
Cumulative-failure auto-cancel still requires real run execution with queued backlog, and the browser flow never progressed beyond persona review / generating variants.
Budget-cap enforcement still depends on admin settings changes plus live execution; the admin settings surface remains inaccessible from the browser.
guardrailEvents auditing remains a backend-table assertion and is still not directly observable from the browser surface.
Subsequent-study effects still require admin settings changes, but the browser surface never exposed an admin-capable settings session.
Credential masking in screenshots/logs still requires a live credentialed run plus artifact inspection, and the fresh browser flow never reached executable runs or artifacts.
Whether encrypted credential payloads are stripped from frontend query responses remains a backend/data-contract assertion rather than a browser-visible behavior.

A fresh browser account could import/publish a pack and create a study, but the live study only reached persona review and the report tab stayed `Report not ready`, so a completed live pack→study→analysis→report pipeline is still not reachable from the browser surface.
Credential-ref leakage still requires a live credentialed study with artifacts to inspect, and the browser flow never reached executable runs or artifacts.
Concurrent-study isolation still requires two live completed studies with separate analysis outputs; this rerun did not reach even one live completed analyzed study from the browser.
Error-recovery integrity still requires inducing an analysis failure and then inspecting preserved run/milestone state, which is not directly observable from the browser surface.

appliedUpdates.description: Recorded hardening rerun findings: demo report JSON/HTML exports now download real files when the browser session has a download path, Copy Link is still blocked by agent-browser clipboard permissions, a fresh imported-pack study can reach persona review but stalls on generating variants, and the launch flow still exposes only high-level confirmation UI.
